mnemonics.title: Staircase Echelon
mnemonics.content: Think of each row as a step; the leading 1 moves right as you go down, like a staircase.

mnemonics.title: Rank = Non‑zero Rows
mnemonics.content: After reducing to echelon form, count the rows that are not all zeros – that count is the rank.

mnemonics.title: Determinant via Triangular
mnemonics.content: If the matrix is upper or lower triangular, the determinant is simply the product of the diagonal entries.

mnemonics.title: Row Swap Sign Flip
mnemonics.content: Every time you swap two rows, the determinant changes sign (multiply by -1).

mnemonics.title: Pivot = Leading 1
mnemonics.content: A pivot (leading 1) is the first non‑zero entry in a non‑zero row; pivots determine the rank.

acronyms.usage: A matrix where each leading non‑zero entry is to the right of the one above; used to solve linear systems.
A unique echelon form where each leading 1 is the only non‑zero entry in its column; simplifies finding solutions and rank.
Expresses a matrix as a product of a lower triangular matrix and an upper triangular matrix, useful for efficient determinant computation.

visualTips.visualization: Draw the matrix as a set of stairs: each row shifted rightward with a highlighted leading 1.
Imagine a building with a number of non‑empty floors equal to the rank; zero rows are empty floors.
In a triangular matrix, shade the diagonal and show the product of those numbers as the determinant.
Place a dot (•) at each pivot in the matrix; the number of dots equals the rank.

analogies.analogy: Rank is like the number of independent directions you can move in a city; if you have 3 independent streets, the rank is 3.
Determinant measures how much a linear transformation stretches or flips area (or volume) – like a rubber sheet being stretched.
Think of a staircase in an old theater: each step (row) starts further right than the previous, making it easy to see where you can stand (pivot).
Like cleaning a messy hallway by pushing dirt (zeros) under each step (pivot) until the floor (matrix) is tidy.
